sanskrit: एतद्योनीनि भूतानि सर्वाणीत्युपधारय । अहं कृत्स्नस्य जगतः प्रभवः प्रलयस्तथा ॥
hindiTranslation: अपरा और परा  इन दोनों प्रकृतियोंके संयोगसे ही सम्पूर्ण प्राणी उत्पन्न होते हैं, ऐसा तुम समझो ।  मैं सम्पूर्ण जगत् का प्रभव तथा प्रलय हूँ ॥
englishTranslation: Know that all beings are born from these two natures. I am the source and the dissolution of the entire universe.
essence: Everything that appears rises from one source and returns there.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: All beings arise from these two natures. Krishna says he is the source and end of the whole universe.
simpleInsight: The world is not random or separate from its source; it rests in a living divine presence.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield, Arjuna is still frozen while Krishna unfolds the hidden structure of reality. After naming lower and higher nature, Krishna says every being comes from their union and that he is both the origin and the end of everything.
modernRelevance: You watch a child, a career, or a relationship begin and end, and it feels fragile. This verse asks you to see the larger ground beneath every rise and collapse.
translationLiteral: एतद्योनीनि (having these as their womb/source) / भूतानि (beings) / सर्वाणि (all) / इति (thus) / उपधारय (understand) / अहम् (I) / कृत्स्नस्य (of the entire) / जगतः (universe) / प्रभवः (source/origin) / प्रलयः (dissolution) / तथा (also)
